reviews.author: Arie den toom
reviews.text: I am sorry but there is only a market on some boats for tourists. Don't believe the pictures they show you! Pictures on the internet from a river full of colorful small boats are from the past. We woke up early for nothing what we did could also be done at 10 in the morning. Use your money for something else.

reviews.author: Yuan Jia
reviews.text: It is a real floating market very close to Can Tho city, not only for tourists. I booked the trip with my friend in the harbour in Can Tho to visit. The average price should be 100k to 500k VND per person, depending on the type of boats and how many places you can visit. It usually starts as early as 5.30 AM, so you need to wake up early if you want to have a good view. This market is recommended by a local friend to me. I took a lot of excellent photos during the trip. You can also buy some souvenirs from some shops, even though the price may be a bit higher.

reviews.author: vu nguyen
reviews.text: Last of the large floating markets in the Mekong. Pretty touristy but also very cool!!! 700k for a private boat for about 3 hours. You can just go to the board walk get a ticket. It's standard pricing.

reviews.author: Geoff Holland
reviews.text: It depends a lot on the tour you do. Big boats are very touristy, but went on a tour with a tour guide on a small boat which allowed us to explore some smaller canals the bigger boats can't get to and also to have a more personalised breakfast from a boat serving beautiful heart soup. Having dumb boat types of tours, a tour guide and smaller boat is the better in my opinion. Went with Tran Ngoc Bich based in Can Tho.

reviews.author: Andreas Harsono
reviews.text: It's a floating market with this fixed location. I saw more than 60 wooden boats, small and big, selling pineapples, durian etc. I am told that they're mostly wholesalers. Some boats were connected, becoming a wider platform where visitors could walk and shop. It's busy early in the morning. I came here at 6am and already busy. It's very intersting to see these boats surviving this crazy era of motorization. It's been here for hundreds of years.

reviews.author: Quentin Thompson
reviews.text: Very little left of what we were told is the last floating market - now it is only wholesale and most business with tourist boats is for food and drink; this is very limited too (mid November 2025). The area of boats stretches about 200 metres when it used to be over 1 kilometre. Could I recommend a visit? For socioeconomic reasons you should go - just to see it's decline and the move to land living, for entertainment you should not. Tours are informative but the use of the waterways as a place to dispose of garbage and assumed bathroom waste from the houses is a reflection of the demographics of this and many regions of Vietnam. What never ceases to amaze and uplift me is the positive cheerful and welcoming attitude of the Vietnamese people. I would come again to Vietnam just for the people.

reviews.author: Andreas K
reviews.text: Mixed feelings. We booked a tour. Our guide was good. But don't expect too much from the market. The promotional photos show more than you can actually see and experience. Still, it's worth visiting the market—you'll have a nice time on the river and can watch some of the trading go on.

reviews.author: Shiela
reviews.text: The photos they post on google and on their adverts are very outdated, so this is like “expectation” vs “reality” We went to the Cai Rang Floating Market to experience their so call “vibrant floating market” hoping we would see the same things as what we saw on google and on the tours advertisements. We took the private boat tour and even went there very early because they said that the market starts at 6am, and it’s better to come early because that is when the floating market are busier but i’ll let you be the judge, I have attached some photos for your reference. Nevertheless, the experience is still cool up to an extent Our tour includes a visit to the Cacao farm (but they ask for entrance fee at the farm whether you are in a tour or just walk-in) And a two more places along the Mekong Delta but it will always depend on your agreement with the tour agents

reviews.author: JB
reviews.text: The floating market was not so special. It was very organized and made only for tourists. The real river markets are probably only deeper inside the country. But the breakfast — Pho soup on the river — was very good. The taste was even better because of the nice atmosphere. Price 2 USD, for the soup.

reviews.author: Annie Ph
reviews.text: These days, the market isn’t as crowded or lively as it used to be. Most of it now is set up for tourists to experience local life. A private boat trip costs around 500k vnd. If you want to save money, you can join a shared boat, but usually need to wait until about 7 AM when enough people show up. It’s worth going early to catch the sunrise,the river view in the morning is peaceful and beautiful. Boats on the river sell quick bites like bun mam and hu tieu. You’ll also find boats selling fruit and handmade local crafts, pretty fun to check out! People here live on their boats and do everything from cooking to trading right there, it’s a unique lifestyle of the Mekong Delta. If you’re in Can Tho, this is definitely something you should experience!

reviews.author: Lee Shaw
reviews.text: Great morning and nice young fella tour guide, studying tourism, spoke well enough English to have a few hours conversation. Pho and coffee on the river was better than our hotel food. Great to see. nice fresh fruit plate, which filled us all up. All the famy enjoyed this excursion at 6am.

reviews.author: Khang Huynh
reviews.text: Cai Rang floating market offers a glimpse into a traditional way of life, even if it caters more to tourists now. While the market isn't the same as it once was due to easier land transportation, it's still a worthwhile experience. We arrived at 5:30 am and enjoyed the cool, fresh morning air. Definitely recommend going early to avoid the heat and strong sun! The food options were decent, but the real highlight was the experience itself. The ticket was 100k and breakfast was 50k

reviews.author: tarah shaw
reviews.text: Amazing trip Absolutely loved this as a family of 4 that love Travel ❤️ A MUST DO when visiting Can Tho. We did the 6am trip, which was encouraged over 7am, as you see more on the river, the earlier the better. First we hoped on our small boat, with driver, guide and family of 4. We went through the narrow canals, which led to the Cai Rang River to then witness the busy waterways of other tourists and floating markets trading. We were served drinks and pho on the river, how awesome. Then had fresh fruits on another boat. We head back down the river to go visit a Traditional Noodle Farm, and how they make Rice Noodles. The Sun came out and we casually made our way back up the narrow canal to finish our 4hr tour. Very informative I am really glad to say I've done this trip as in years to come the floating markets will finish forever ❤️

reviews.author: Hằng
reviews.text: It’s just a new experience for tourists, I think locals would not visit floating markets for buying things. We had our breakfast here, it’s not delicious. We also bought local specialties and fruits. Although the market is just a tour, and you just see tourists here, it’s still a must-visit place in Can Tho city.

reviews.author: Christopher Park
reviews.text: Probably more tourist boats than actual market boats - still it’s a nice ride on the water in the cool morning air watching the sun rise. A relief from the sweltering midday sun! The tours will insist that you start at an ungodly 5AM! You’ll be racing down river with all the other party boats with tourists in bright orange life vests 😂 But it’s Can Tho and you Have to do it right? Just book the cheapest tour ‘cause for the locals it’s all about the money not the actual market! And enjoy the boat ride!

reviews.author: Alice Kingsleigh
reviews.text: As a tourist, I found it quite fascinating! A brand new experience that I have never had in my life. Never saw anything like it. Unfortunately, I can give you any info on the wholesale prices cause I only bought a cup of coffee from a gentleman who much like a pirate (friendly one) boarded our boat 😁

reviews.author: Christa Brazier
reviews.text: The Chamg Ray Floating Market offers a poignant glimpse into a tradition that is, sadly, very much a dying attraction. While it remains a presence for tourists, visitors should expect to find only a handful of boats still actively engaged in trade. The balance has shifted, with more vessels now focusing on offering refreshments rather than selling goods in the traditional sense. However, the market still manages to offer an impression of its history, and the sight of the old wooden vessels alone makes it worth observing. It’s an experience that feels preserved primarily to keep the memory alive for visitors, a quiet echo of a vibrant past.

reviews.author: Jimmy Farrow
reviews.text: Get there whilst you can. Our tour guide said there used to be 15 floating markets, but with the increase of bridges and modern vehicles, there is only 1 left. This market is subsidised by the government yo try and keep it alive. It's certainly an experience worth having a look at.

reviews.author: Vlad Blaj
reviews.text: There is no more floating market. We have accommodation near the market so we are able to quickly get to it. 99% of the boats are tourist boats. We have seen exactly one boat selling pineaples and one selling coconuts. Don’t waste your day waking up at 5am. There is no market anymore, all trades happen on land.

reviews.author: Michael Case
reviews.text: After a very early start we travelled by boat to this floating market. Enjoyable boat ride but the market was very underwhelming. More tourist boats than market boats! This tourist attraction may be in decline as the real market activity is increasingly on land.

reviews.author: Birgit Nakamhela
reviews.text: We had a great tourguide. She showed us around and explained everything. We had breakfast and a great coffee on the boat. The floating market is an absolute highlight although one could think it only exists because of the tourists. Sad is the trash in the water. In the long run it could make a difference to the people if the rubbish could be removed professionally and not land in the water.

reviews.author: Den Leo
reviews.text: Cai Rang Floating Market is one of the most popular tourist attractions in Can Tho. We woke up early to enjoy a pleasant boat ride to the market. Although it now caters more to tourists than serving as a traditional market, it was still enjoyable to see a part of the traditional lifestyle in the Mekong Delta, where people sell food and fruit directly from their boats.

reviews.author: Linh Nguyen
reviews.text: Beautiful view, friendly people in here. Good food 😍😍

reviews.author: Inspired4me2You
reviews.text: Unmissable Tour- Floating Markets We were picked up at 4am!! 3 hours to get there! Absolutely worth it. A fantastic experience. The market buzzing and we did a few other activities including making rice paper. Families for older children say 10 + Great for couples. We booked a private tour so just us. Highly recommend this as a must do when visiting Vietnam. Love your local guide- ❤️ Follow me for more reviews

reviews.author: Benson J. Langford
reviews.text: 🚤 I FINALLY experienced Cái Răng Floating Market and WOW! Waking up at 5 AM was SO worth it - watching hundreds of colorful boats trading fresh fruits and veggies at sunrise was absolutely magical! Small vendor boats pulled up alongside ours selling breakfast - I had fresh pho on the water! This century-old tradition is pure Mekong Delta soul! 😍🌅

reviews.author: Martha (The real reviewer)
reviews.text: 🛶🛶 Floating Market tips 🛶🛶 We stayed in Can Tho and were quoted extortionate amounts of 1.5 mill dong for a trip to the market, noodle factory, canal and orchard. Along the front of the river near the night market there were friendly ladies selling boat tours the exact same as our hotel for 300k per person on a private boat, leaving at 5am. We were slightly sceptical, however after seeing groups at the above attractions on our tour we realised they would be having an identical experience. The tour itself was okay, I wouldn’t hold any expectations. The main enjoyment was the actual boat and being on the river. After a 4 hour tour, we felt that 550k for 2 people was a fair price, anything higher I would have felt ripped off.

reviews.author: Mark Df
reviews.text: We were here just after sunrise and there is a lot of activity, nice to see. luckily we were in the area. I wouldn't have traveled all the way from Ho Chi Minh, it's definitely not worth it.

reviews.author: David Llopis
reviews.text: An interesting place to visit. I visited the market in January and in the Tet celebration, new year. The market is full of tourist, you can breakfast in the boats and buy some food to the local sellers. At the end is something a bit tourist and unfortunately the water of the entire river is quite dirty but it's an interesting experience.

reviews.author: N Harris
reviews.text: To experience the fun of the river area, you have to get up early and travel by boat for about 30 minutes from the center to the floating market, the market sells many fruits, as well as specialties of the western river area, the prices are reasonable. The people are very friendly and cheerful

reviews.author: Russ Hud
reviews.text: Very disappointing it’s just tourist boats sailing around a marina. A few barges are carrying produce to be unloaded and a few small boats selling coffee and water. It has its charm as all marinas do but avoid sitting on a boat breathing in diesel stay in bed it’s not worth it
